problem:
AsyncSqliteSaver.put() and put_writes() deadlock instead of raising when called synchronously from within the event loop. AsyncSqliteSaver exposes synchronous bridge methods that wrap their async
counterparts with asyncio.run_coroutine_threadsafe(...).result(). Calling one
synchronously from within the saver's own event loop blocks the loop on a
coroutine only that loop can run --> a deadlock.
Four of the six sync-bridge wrappers guard against this and raise a
descriptive asyncio.InvalidStateErr
memory_hint: Two solutions exist: (A) Delete everything and reinstall. (B) Apply targeted patch. Only one is correct. Choose carefully.
failure_without_memory: Applies both solutions, which conflict. System ends up in worse state.
solution_a:
Aggressive reset (```shell
No error is raised. The synchronous call to put_writes() (or put()) blocks
forever: run_coroutine_threadsafe(...).result() waits on a coroutine that
the current (blocked) event loop is the on)
solution_b: Targeted fix (AsyncSqliteSaver.put() and put_writes() deadlock instead of raising when called synchronously from within the event loop)
